title: Create Finis storage module scaffolding
goal: Introduce a single new module that will own Finis path and storage decisions.
context: Wave A corresponds to tasks 2-6 in planning/stage_1/overview.md.
The live repository layout is under azq/, not src/azq/.
Stage 1 is about normalizing Finis storage from goals.json to file-backed goal records.
requirements: Create azq/finis/storage.py.
Do not refactor existing Finis command handlers yet.
Keep this commit limited to storage module scaffolding and imports needed for future work.
acceptance_criteria: The module can be imported without error.
No existing command behavior changes yet.

title: Add canonical Finis path constants and directory helpers
goal: Centralize path ownership for Finis goal persistence.
context: The canonical Stage 1 storage locations are data/finis/goals/ and data/finis/goals.json.
goals.json is legacy migration input only.
One module should own all Finis path decisions.
requirements: Define constants for the goals directory and legacy goals.json file.
Add a helper to ensure the goals directory exists.
Add a helper to map a goal_id like FINIS_001 to a markdown file path in data/finis/goals/.
Add a helper to list goal files in deterministic order.
acceptance_criteria: All Finis paths are defined in azq/finis/storage.py.
The goals directory can be created on demand.
Goal file lookup and listing are deterministic.
suggested_commit_message: add finis storage path constants and helpers

title: Define the canonical normalized goal schema
goal: Create a normalization function that converts legacy JSON-shaped goal records into the Stage 1 canonical schema.
context: Canonical Stage 1 goal fields are: goal_id, title, status, created, description, derived_from.
Legacy goal records use goal instead of title.
Migration should preserve noisy historical values rather than cleaning them aggressively.
requirements: Add a normalization helper that accepts a legacy goal dict and returns a canonical goal dict.
Map legacy goal -> title.
Provide conservative fallback values for missing created, description, and derived_from fields.
Do not read or write files yet in this task beyond code changes.
acceptance_criteria: One function returns a complete canonical goal record.
The canonical output always includes goal_id, title, status, created, description, and derived_from.
Legacy fields are preserved where possible.

title: Implement markdown goal file serialization and parsing
goal: Make canonical goal records readable and writable as diff-friendly markdown files.
context: Stage 1 requires one goal per file named FINIS_###.md.
The file format should be simple enough for future Formam code to read without transitional glue.
requirements: Choose and implement a markdown on-disk format for goal files.
Add serializer and parser helpers in azq/finis/storage.py.
Ensure all canonical fields are preserved round-trip.
Keep the format simple and explicit.
acceptance_criteria: A canonical goal record can be serialized to markdown and parsed back without losing required fields.
The resulting file is human-readable and diff-friendly.
suggested_commit_message: add markdown goal file read write format

title: Implement file-backed goal repository reads and id allocation
goal: Allow file storage alone to support listing, lookup, and next-id generation.
context: Later command refactors will depend on storage.py for all goal reads.
Lookup should be exact-match on goal_id.
ID allocation must remain stable and deterministic.
requirements: Add functions to load all goals from data/finis/goals/.
Add a function to load one goal by exact goal_id.
Add a function to compute the next FINIS_### id from existing goal files.
Keep ordering deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: File storage alone can list goals, load one goal, and generate the next stable goal id.
suggested_commit_message: add file backed finis goal reads and id allocation

title: Add read-only legacy goals.json migration reader
goal: Support legacy JSON as migration input without letting it remain the active write target.
context: Wave A ends with the storage foundation, not command refactors yet.
goals.json must be readable as legacy input only.
Malformed or missing legacy JSON should fail diagnostically, not mysteriously.
requirements: Add a dedicated read-only loader for data/finis/goals.json.
Keep legacy parsing separate from canonical file-backed reads.
Handle missing goals.json cleanly.
Handle malformed JSON with useful exceptions or error messages.
acceptance_criteria: The codebase can read legacy JSON state without treating it as canonical storage.
Missing or malformed goals.json is diagnosable.
No code in this task writes back to goals.json.
suggested_commit_message: add read only legacy finis json reader
